content.text: Transformation: trim each field, uppercase, reverse field order, `,`→`;`, append `;OK`. Let me write and test the script.

edits.new:
call setreg('a', ":s/^ *\\| *$//g\<CR>:s/ *, */;/g\<CR>")
call setreg('b', "gUU")
call setreg('c', ":s/\\(.*\\);\\(.*\\);\\(.*\\)/\\3;\\2;\\1;OK/\<CR>")
:%normal! @a
:%normal! @b
:%normal! @c
:wq

input.command: cd /app && head -100 input.csv > /tmp/sample.csv && head -100 expected.csv > /tmp/sample_exp.csv && cp /tmp/sample.csv /tmp/test.csv && vim -Nu NONE -n -Es /tmp/test.csv -S /app/apply_macros.vim; echo "exit=$?" && diff /tmp/test.csv /tmp/sample_exp.csv && echo "SAMPLE MATCH"

content.text: Sample matches. Now the full 1M-row run:

input.command: cd /app && cp input.csv /tmp/full.csv && time vim -Nu NONE -n -Es /tmp/full.csv -S /app/apply_macros.vim; echo "exit=$?" && cmp /tmp/full.csv expected.csv && echo "FULL BYTE-MATCH"

content.text:
Verified: exit 0, byte-for-byte match with expected.csv on the full 1M rows (28s, well within limits).

Final `/app/apply_macros.vim`:
- **`@a`** (28 keys): `:s/^ *\| *$//g` trims line ends, then `:s/ *, */;/g` collapses `,`+surrounding spaces into `;`
- **`@b`** (3 keys): `gUU` uppercases the line
- **`@c`** (37 keys): `:s/\(.*\);\(.*\);\(.*\)/\3;\2;\1;OK/` reverses the three fields and appends `;OK`

Total **68 keystrokes** across three distinct non-empty macros (<200). Script contains only `call setreg(...)`, `:%normal! @x`, and `:wq`. Ran on a copy (`/tmp/full.csv`) to leave `/app/input.csv` intact for the grader.
